name: Veal in Apple-Lemon Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Pat the veal dry with paper towels. Place between sheets of waxed paper and flatten with a mallet until about 1/8-inch thick.
2. Dredge in the flour. Shake off the excess.
3. In a 2-quart saucepan, cook the apples in the lemon juice until tender, about 5 to 7 minutes.
4. Puree half of the apples in a blender. Reserve the remaining slices separately.
5. In a large nonstick frying pan, heat 2 tablespoons olive oil over medium heat. Add the mushrooms and saute until tender, about 5 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
6. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons olive oil to the pan. Saute the veal about 1 1/2 minutes on each side. Remove the veal to a platter and keep warm.
7. Add the chicken stock to the pan and bring to a boil, scraping the bottom of the pan to loosen browned bits. Lower the heat, add the apple puree, and cook for 2 to 3 minutes.
8. Add the veal, mushrooms, and apple slices back into the pan. Cook for an additional 5 minutes.
